title: post-deploy verification + fix website-ingress namespace label
description:
## Summary
- **`scripts/verify-deployment.sh`**: new script that probes critical cross-namespace network paths (websiteâ†’keycloak, workspaceâ†’website API, workspaceâ†’shared-db) and validates that `workspace-secrets` credentials are not stuck as SEALED placeholders. Reports readiness of core deployments.
- **`Taskfile.yml`**: `workspace:verify` / `workspace:verify:all-prods` tasks (wired into `feature:deploy` and `task health`); `workspace:admin-users-setup:all-prods` for one-shot credential provisioning on both clusters.
- **`k3d/network-policies.yaml`**: fix `allow-website-ingress` `namespaceSelector` â€” `"korczewski-website"` was wrong, correct name is `"website-korczewski"`. Without this, korczewski website pods were silently blocked from calling workspace services.

## Why
We hit two cross-namespace connectivity issues tonight that could only be found by manually reading pod logs after login failures. This tooling surfaces them automatically after every deploy.

## Test plan
- [ ] `task workspace:validate` passes (CI)
- [ ] `task workspace:verify ENV=mentolder` â€” all checks green on live cluster
- [ ] `task workspace:verify ENV=korczewski` â€” all checks green on live cluster
